# stochtree R package

Software for building stochastic tree ensembles (i.e. BART, XBART) for
supervised learning and causal inference.

## Getting started

`stochtree` can be installed from CRAN via

    install.packages("stochtree")

The development version of stochtree can be installed from github via

    remotes::install_github("StochasticTree/stochtree", ref="r-dev")

# Package index

## Supervised learning

High-level functionality for training supervised Bayesian tree ensembles
(BART, XBART)

- [`bart()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/bart.md) : Run the BART
  algorithm for supervised learning.

- [`predict(`*`<bartmodel>`*`)`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/predict.bartmodel.md)
  : Predict from a sampled BART model on new data

- [`compute_bart_posterior_interval()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/compute_bart_posterior_interval.md)
  : Compute posterior credible intervals for specified terms from a
  fitted BART model. It supports intervals for mean functions, variance
  functions, random effects, and overall predictions.

- [`compute_contrast_bart_model()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/compute_contrast_bart_model.md)
  :

  Compute a contrast using a BART model by making two sets of outcome
  predictions and taking their difference. This function provides the
  flexibility to compute any contrast of interest by specifying
  covariates, leaf basis, and random effects bases / IDs for both sides
  of a two term contrast. For simplicity, we refer to the subtrahend of
  the contrast as the "control" or `Y0` term and the minuend of the
  contrast as the `Y1` term, though the requested contrast need not
  match the "control vs treatment" terminology of a classic
  two-treatment causal inference problem. We mirror the function calls
  and terminology of the `predict.bartmodel` function, labeling each
  prediction data term with a `1` to denote its contribution to the
  treatment prediction of a contrast and `0` to denote inclusion in the
  control prediction.

- [`sample_bart_posterior_predictive()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/sample_bart_posterior_predictive.md)
  : Sample from the posterior predictive distribution for outcomes
  modeled by BART

## Causal inference

High-level functionality for estimating causal effects using Bayesian
tree ensembles (BCF, XBCF)

- [`bcf()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/bcf.md) : Run the Bayesian
  Causal Forest (BCF) algorithm for regularized causal effect
  estimation.

- [`predict(`*`<bcfmodel>`*`)`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/predict.bcfmodel.md)
  : Predict from a sampled BCF model on new data

- [`compute_bcf_posterior_interval()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/compute_bcf_posterior_interval.md)
  : Compute posterior credible intervals for BCF model terms

- [`compute_contrast_bcf_model()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/compute_contrast_bcf_model.md)
  :

  Compute a contrast using a BCF model by making two sets of outcome
  predictions and taking their difference. For simple BCF models with
  binary treatment, this will yield the same prediction as requesting
  `terms = "cate"` in the `predict.bcfmodel` function. For more general
  models, such as models with continuous / multivariate treatments or an
  additive random effects term with a coefficient on the treatment, this
  function provides the flexibility to compute a any contrast of
  interest by specifying covariates, treatment, and random effects bases
  and IDs for both sides of a two term contrast. For simplicity, we
  refer to the subtrahend of the contrast as the "control" or `Y0` term
  and the minuend of the contrast as the `Y1` term, though the requested
  contrast need not match the "control vs treatment" terminology of a
  classic two-arm experiment. We mirror the function calls and
  terminology of the `predict.bcfmodel` function, labeling each
  prediction data term with a `1` to denote its contribution to the
  treatment prediction of a contrast and `0` to denote inclusion in the
  control prediction.

- [`sample_bcf_posterior_predictive()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/sample_bcf_posterior_predictive.md)
  : Sample from the posterior predictive distribution for outcomes
  modeled by BCF

## Low-level functionality

### Serialization

Classes and functions for converting sampling artifacts to JSON and
saving to disk

- [`CppJson`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/CppJson.md) : Class that
  stores draws from an random ensemble of decision trees
- [`createCppJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createCppJson.md) :
  Create a new (empty) C++ Json object
- [`createCppJsonFile()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createCppJsonFile.md)
  : Create a C++ Json object from a Json file
- [`createCppJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createCppJsonString.md)
  : Create a C++ Json object from a Json string
- [`loadForestContainerJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadForestContainerJson.md)
  : Load a container of forest samples from json
- [`loadForestContainerCombinedJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadForestContainerCombinedJson.md)
  : Combine multiple JSON model objects containing forests (with the
  same hierarchy / schema) into a single forest_container
- [`loadForestContainerCombinedJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadForestContainerCombinedJsonString.md)
  : Combine multiple JSON strings representing model objects containing
  forests (with the same hierarchy / schema) into a single
  forest_container
- [`loadVectorJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadVectorJson.md)
  : Load a vector from json
- [`loadScalarJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadScalarJson.md)
  : Load a scalar from json
- [`loadRandomEffectSamplesJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadRandomEffectSamplesJson.md)
  : Load a container of random effect samples from json
- [`loadRandomEffectSamplesCombinedJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadRandomEffectSamplesCombinedJson.md)
  : Combine multiple JSON model objects containing random effects (with
  the same hierarchy / schema) into a single container
- [`loadRandomEffectSamplesCombinedJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/loadRandomEffectSamplesCombinedJsonString.md)
  : Combine multiple JSON strings representing model objects containing
  random effects (with the same hierarchy / schema) into a single
  container
- [`saveBARTModelToJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/saveBARTModelToJson.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a BART model to (in-memory) JSON
- [`saveBARTModelToJsonFile()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/saveBARTModelToJsonFile.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a BART model to (in-memory) JSON
  and save to a file
- [`saveBARTModelToJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/saveBARTModelToJsonString.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a BART model to (in-memory) JSON
  string
- [`createBARTModelFromJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBARTModelFromJson.md)
  : Convert an (in-memory) JSON representation of a BART model to a BART
  model object which can be used for prediction, etc...
- [`createBARTModelFromJsonFile()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBARTModelFromJsonFile.md)
  : Convert a JSON file containing sample information on a trained BART
  model to a BART model object which can be used for prediction, etc...
- [`createBARTModelFromJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBARTModelFromJsonString.md)
  : Convert a JSON string containing sample information on a trained
  BART model to a BART model object which can be used for prediction,
  etc...
- [`createBARTModelFromCombinedJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBARTModelFromCombinedJson.md)
  : Convert a list of (in-memory) JSON representations of a BART model
  to a single combined BART model object which can be used for
  prediction, etc...
- [`createBARTModelFromCombinedJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBARTModelFromCombinedJsonString.md)
  : Convert a list of (in-memory) JSON strings that represent BART
  models to a single combined BART model object which can be used for
  prediction, etc...
- [`saveBCFModelToJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/saveBCFModelToJson.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a BCF model to (in-memory) JSON
- [`saveBCFModelToJsonFile()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/saveBCFModelToJsonFile.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a BCF model to (in-memory) JSON
  and save to a file
- [`saveBCFModelToJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/saveBCFModelToJsonString.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a BCF model to (in-memory) JSON
  string
- [`createBCFModelFromJsonFile()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBCFModelFromJsonFile.md)
  : Convert a JSON file containing sample information on a trained BCF
  model to a BCF model object which can be used for prediction, etc...
- [`createBCFModelFromJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBCFModelFromJsonString.md)
  : Convert a JSON string containing sample information on a trained BCF
  model to a BCF model object which can be used for prediction, etc...
- [`createBCFModelFromJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBCFModelFromJson.md)
  : Convert an (in-memory) JSON representation of a BCF model to a BCF
  model object which can be used for prediction, etc...
- [`createBCFModelFromCombinedJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBCFModelFromCombinedJson.md)
  : Convert a list of (in-memory) JSON strings that represent BCF models
  to a single combined BCF model object which can be used for
  prediction, etc...
- [`createBCFModelFromCombinedJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createBCFModelFromCombinedJsonString.md)
  : Convert a list of (in-memory) JSON strings that represent BCF models
  to a single combined BCF model object which can be used for
  prediction, etc...

### Data

Classes and functions for preparing data for sampling algorithms

- [`ForestDataset`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/ForestDataset.md) :
  Dataset used to sample a forest
- [`createForestDataset()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createForestDataset.md)
  : Create a forest dataset object
- [`Outcome`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/Outcome.md) : Outcome /
  partial residual used to sample an additive model.
- [`createOutcome()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createOutcome.md) :
  Create an outcome object
- [`RandomEffectsDataset`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/RandomEffectsDataset.md)
  : Dataset used to sample a random effects model
- [`createRandomEffectsDataset()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createRandomEffectsDataset.md)
  : Create a random effects dataset object
- [`preprocessTrainData()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/preprocessTrainData.md)
  : Preprocess covariates. DataFrames will be preprocessed based on
  their column types. Matrices will be passed through assuming all
  columns are numeric.
- [`preprocessPredictionData()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/preprocessPredictionData.md)
  : Preprocess covariates. DataFrames will be preprocessed based on
  their column types. Matrices will be passed through assuming all
  columns are numeric.
- [`convertPreprocessorToJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/convertPreprocessorToJson.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a covariate preprocessor to
  (in-memory) C++ JSON object
- [`savePreprocessorToJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/savePreprocessorToJsonString.md)
  : Convert the persistent aspects of a covariate preprocessor to
  (in-memory) JSON string
- [`createPreprocessorFromJson()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createPreprocessorFromJson.md)
  : Reload a covariate preprocessor object from a JSON string containing
  a serialized preprocessor
- [`createPreprocessorFromJsonString()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createPreprocessorFromJsonString.md)
  : Reload a covariate preprocessor object from a JSON string containing
  a serialized preprocessor

### Forest

Classes and functions for constructing and persisting forests

- [`Forest`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/Forest.md) : Class that
  stores a single ensemble of decision trees (often treated as the
  "active forest")

- [`createForest()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createForest.md) :
  Create a forest

- [`ForestModel`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/ForestModel.md) : Class
  that defines and samples a forest model

- [`createForestModel()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createForestModel.md)
  : Create a forest model object

- [`ForestSamples`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/ForestSamples.md) :
  Class that stores draws from an random ensemble of decision trees

- [`createForestSamples()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createForestSamples.md)
  : Create a container of forest samples

- [`ForestModelConfig`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/ForestModelConfig.md)
  : Object used to get / set parameters and other model configuration
  options for a forest model in the "low-level" stochtree interface

- [`createForestModelConfig()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createForestModelConfig.md)
  : Create a forest model config object

- [`GlobalModelConfig`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/GlobalModelConfig.md)
  : Object used to get / set global parameters and other global model
  configuration options in the "low-level" stochtree interface

- [`createGlobalModelConfig()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createGlobalModelConfig.md)
  : Create a global model config object

- [`CppRNG`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/CppRNG.md) : Class that
  wraps a C++ random number generator (for reproducibility)

- [`createCppRNG()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createCppRNG.md) :
  Create an R class that wraps a C++ random number generator

- [`calibrateInverseGammaErrorVariance()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/calibrateInverseGammaErrorVariance.md)
  : Calibrate the scale parameter on an inverse gamma prior for the
  global error variance as in Chipman et al (2022)

- [`computeForestMaxLeafIndex()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/computeForestMaxLeafIndex.md)
  :

  Compute and return the largest possible leaf index computable by
  `computeForestLeafIndices` for the forests in a designated forest
  sample container.

- [`computeForestLeafIndices()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/computeForestLeafIndices.md)
  : Compute vector of forest leaf indices

- [`computeForestLeafVariances()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/computeForestLeafVariances.md)
  : Compute vector of forest leaf scale parameters

- [`resetActiveForest()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/resetActiveForest.md)
  :

  Reset an active forest, either from a specific forest in a
  `ForestContainer` or to an ensemble of single-node (i.e. root) trees

- [`resetForestModel()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/resetForestModel.md)
  :

  Re-initialize a forest model (tracking data structures) from a
  specific forest in a `ForestContainer`

### Random Effects

Classes and functions for constructing and persisting random effects
terms

- [`RandomEffectSamples`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/RandomEffectSamples.md)
  : Class that wraps the "persistent" aspects of a C++ random effects
  model (draws of the parameters and a map from the original label
  indices to the 0-indexed label numbers used to place group samples in
  memory (i.e. the first label is stored in column 0 of the sample
  matrix, the second label is store in column 1 of the sample matrix,
  etc...))

- [`createRandomEffectSamples()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createRandomEffectSamples.md)
  :

  Create a `RandomEffectSamples` object

- [`RandomEffectsModel`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/RandomEffectsModel.md)
  : The core "model" class for sampling random effects.

- [`createRandomEffectsModel()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createRandomEffectsModel.md)
  :

  Create a `RandomEffectsModel` object

- [`RandomEffectsTracker`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/RandomEffectsTracker.md)
  : Class that defines a "tracker" for random effects models, most
  notably storing the data indices available in each group for quicker
  posterior computation and sampling of random effects terms.

- [`createRandomEffectsTracker()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/createRandomEffectsTracker.md)
  :

  Create a `RandomEffectsTracker` object

- [`getRandomEffectSamples()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/getRandomEffectSamples.md)
  : Generic function for extracting random effect samples from a model
  object (BCF, BART, etc...)

- [`getRandomEffectSamples(`*`<bartmodel>`*`)`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/getRandomEffectSamples.bartmodel.md)
  : Extract raw sample values for each of the random effect parameter
  terms.

- [`getRandomEffectSamples(`*`<bcfmodel>`*`)`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/getRandomEffectSamples.bcfmodel.md)
  : Extract raw sample values for each of the random effect parameter
  terms.

- [`sampleGlobalErrorVarianceOneIteration()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/sampleGlobalErrorVarianceOneIteration.md)
  : Sample one iteration of the (inverse gamma) global variance model

- [`sampleLeafVarianceOneIteration()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/sampleLeafVarianceOneIteration.md)
  : Sample one iteration of the leaf parameter variance model (only for
  univariate basis and constant leaf!)

- [`resetRandomEffectsModel()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/resetRandomEffectsModel.md)
  :

  Reset a `RandomEffectsModel` object based on the parameters indexed by
  `sample_num` in a `RandomEffectsSamples` object

- [`resetRandomEffectsTracker()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/resetRandomEffectsTracker.md)
  :

  Reset a `RandomEffectsTracker` object based on the parameters indexed
  by `sample_num` in a `RandomEffectsSamples` object

- [`rootResetRandomEffectsModel()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/rootResetRandomEffectsModel.md)
  :

  Reset a `RandomEffectsModel` object to its "default" state

- [`rootResetRandomEffectsTracker()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/rootResetRandomEffectsTracker.md)
  :

  Reset a `RandomEffectsTracker` object to its "default" state

### Utilities

Miscellaneous “utility” classes and functions

- [`sample_without_replacement()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/sample_without_replacement.md)
  :

  Draw `sample_size` samples from `population_vector` without
  replacement, weighted by `sampling_probabilities`

- [`expand_dims_1d()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/expand_dims_1d.md)
  :

  Convert scalar input to vector of dimension `output_size`, or check
  that input array is equivalent to a vector of dimension `output_size`.

- [`expand_dims_2d()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/expand_dims_2d.md)
  :

  Ensures that input is propagated appropriately to a matrix of
  dimension `output_rows` x `output_cols`. Handles the following cases:

  1.  `input` is a scalar: output is simply a (`output_rows`,
      `output_cols`) matrix with `input` repeated for each element

  2.  `input` is a vector of length `output_rows`: output is a
      (`output_rows`, `output_cols`) array with `input` broadcast across
      each of `output_cols` columns

  3.  `input` is a vector of length `output_cols`: output is a
      (`output_rows`, `output_cols`) array with `input` broadcast across
      each of `output_rows` rows

  4.  `input` is a matrix of dimension (`output_rows`, `output_cols`):
      input is passed through as-is All other cases throw an error.

- [`expand_dims_2d_diag()`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/expand_dims_2d_diag.md)
  :

  Convert scalar input to square matrix of dimension `output_size` x
  `output_size` with `input` along the diagonal, or check that input
  array is equivalent to a square matrix of dimension `output_size` x
  `output_size`.

## Package info

High-level package details

- [`stochtree`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/stochtree-package.md)
  [`stochtree-package`](https://stochtree.ai/reference/stochtree-package.md)
  : stochtree: Stochastic Tree Ensembles (XBART and BART) for Supervised
  Learning and Causal Inference

# Articles

### High-Level Model Fitting

- [Bayesian Supervised Learning in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/BayesianSupervisedLearning.md):
- [Causal Machine Learning in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/CausalInference.md):
- [Bayesian Supervised Learning with Heteroskedasticity in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/Heteroskedasticity.md):

### Advanced Model Interface

- [Running Multiple Chains (Sequentially or in Parallel) in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/MultiChain.md):
- [Model Serialization in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/ModelSerialization.md):
- [Prior Calibration Approaches for Parametric Components of Stochastic
  Tree Ensembles](https://stochtree.ai/articles/PriorCalibration.md):
- [Kernel Methods from Tree Ensembles in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/EnsembleKernel.md):
- [Deeper Dive on Sampled Forests in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/TreeInspection.md):

### Prototype Interface

- [Custom Sampling Routines in
  StochTree](https://stochtree.ai/articles/CustomSamplingRoutine.md):
